---
title: Code cells
---

Course material may show code for reading, execute code while rendering, or
produce interactive notebooks. The syntax distinguishes displayed examples
from executable cells.

## Static code

Use an ordinary fenced block when code should be read or copied but not run by
Quarto:

````markdown
```python
def concentration(amount, volume):
    return amount / volume
```
````

This provides syntax highlighting only, the cell above for example produces the following 
syntax-highlighted code 

```python
def concentration(amount, volume):
    return amount / volume
```

## Executable Python

### Configuration

In order to include executable Python code in a document the project must have a 
Python execution engine, this means 

- Having Python declared as a dependency in `pixi.toml`
- Any packages used by the Python code need to be declared in `pixi.toml`. 

An example with a mimimal dependency set is shown below

```default
[dependencies]
quarto = ">=1.9.38,<2"
python = "==3.12"

[pypi-dependencies]
jupyter = ">=1.1.1, <2"
```

Additional packages can be added using 

```default
pixi add <package-name> --pypi
```

### Example

When the project is configured with a Python execution engine, braces mark a
cell for execution:

```{{python}}
amount = 0.004
volume = 0.2
amount / volume
print(volume)
```

This code will be executed during rendering, producing highlighted code and output like below 

```{python}
amount = 0.004
volume = 0.2
amount / volume
print(volume)
```

Execution behavior, cached outputs, and output formats are controlled by
document front matter and `_quarto.yml`.

## Learner and answer cells

For new projects with the `strip-solution` teaching-tools extension, mark
conditional cells with the preferred `teaching` option:

<pre><code>```&#123;python&#125;
#| teaching: exercise
#| eval: false
result = ...  # learner completes this cell
```

```&#123;python&#125;
#| teaching: solution
result = 0.004 / 0.2
result
```</code></pre>

With `teaching.show-solutions: false`, the student render removes the
solution cell. With `teaching.show-solutions: true`, the instructor render
removes the exercise cell. Mark student scaffold cells with `#| eval: false`
when they should not execute; the extension does not do this automatically.

::: {.callout-note title="Existing material"}
The extension still accepts legacy `#| exercise: true` and
`#| solution: true` options for existing repositories. Use the `teaching`
option for new authoring.
:::

## Verification

When editing executable material, verify that:

- The relevant profile includes the intended cells.
- Code executes without errors in rendered outputs.
- A learner-facing cell leaves an appropriate starting point.
- A generated notebook contains the cells and outputs learners are expected to
  use.
